meaning: adj.繁重的,烦累的,难以承担的
examples: As the report authors point out, even near-misses might lead to the "creation of a burdensome regulatory framework and affect the future disposition of science Cube Sats".
In many cases, it would not be overly burdensome for authorities to obtain a warrant to search through phone contents.
Employees are an expense item, one that becomes increasingly burdensome when demand dries up.
It has never been much fun, he reflects, but now it seems particularly burdensome.
